meta.title: Is a Nuclear Iran Inevitable?
meta.description: For the last ten months, Iran has expanded its nuclear infrastructure and highly-enriched stockpiles in dangerous ways and rebuffed inspections. It is now increasingly apparent that the world’s leading state sponsor of terrorism could possess nuclear weapons within a little as one month. What can the United States and its partners do to prevent this terrifying development? Nuclear experts David Albright and Olli Heinonen join Hudson fellow Josh Block for the latest in a series of conversations on Iran’s nuclear ambitions.
